# glitchtip_project_key (Resource)

Manages a GlitchTip project key (DSN). A project key provides the public authentication string used to ingest events into a project.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "glitchtip_project_key" "example" {
  organization = glitchtip_organization.example.slug
  project      = glitchtip_project.example.slug
  name         = "production"

  rate_limit = {
    window = 60
    count  = 1000
  }
}

output "dsn" {
  value     = glitchtip_project_key.example.dsn
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `organization` (String) Slug of the organization that owns the project. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `project` (String) Slug of the project the key belongs to. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `name` (String) Human-readable label for the key. The GlitchTip API does not support changing a key's label after creation, so changing it forces a new key (and a new DSN) to be created.
- `rate_limit` (Attributes) Optional rate limit applied to event ingestion through this key. When omitted, the key is not rate limited. The GlitchTip API does not support changing the rate limit after creation, so changing it forces a new key to be created. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rate_limit))

### Read-Only

- `date_created` (String) RFC 3339 timestamp at which the key was created.
- `dsn` (Map of String) Map of DSN endpoints (e.g. `public`, `secret`, `security`) for this key.
- `id` (String) UUID identifier of the project key.
- `project_id` (Number) Numeric identifier of the project the key belongs to.
- `public` (String) Public key portion of the DSN.

<a id="nestedatt--rate_limit"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rate_limit`

Required:

- `count` (Number) Maximum number of events permitted within the window.
- `window` (Number) Length of the rate-limit window in seconds.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Project keys are imported as "<organization-slug>/<project-slug>/<key-id>".
terraform import glitchtip_project_key.example acme-inc/api/2f1c0d9a8b7e4f3a9c1d2e3f4a5b6c7d
```
